message:
perf(types): optimize ASCII case-insensitive string comparison for LIKE/CONTAINS

Rewrite the core byte-level comparison functions used by SQL LIKE pattern
matching to avoid iterator overhead:

- ascii_ci_eq_bytes: replace iter().zip().all() with a manual while loop
  that short-circuits on first mismatch — eliminates iterator allocation
  and function-pointer dispatch per byte
- ascii_ci_contains: replace windows().any() linear scan with a
  first-byte-filtered search that skips positions where the first byte
  cannot match, reducing the number of full-length comparisons
- Extract ascii_fold_byte and ascii_ci_eq_byte as inline helpers

Add regression tests:
- test_like_contains_fast_path_handles_overlapping_matches: verifies
  %ana% correctly matches "bananas" (overlapping pattern)
- test_like_contains_fast_path_preserves_non_ascii_byte_matching: verifies
  %ß% matches "straße" and %SS% does not (non-ASCII byte preserved)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
